mission.title: Install and Configure Openfeature on Kubernetes
mission.description: OpenFeature is an open specification that provides a vendor-agnostic, community-driven API for feature flagging. Installing OpenFeature allows you to integrate feature flag management into your applications seamlessly.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the OpenFeature Helm repository to your local Helm configuration using the following command:
```bash
helm repo add open-feature-operator https://open-feature.github.io/open-feature-operator
```

steps.title: Update the Helm repository
steps.description:
Update your local Helm repository to ensure you have the latest charts:
```bash
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install OpenFeature
steps.description:
Install the OpenFeature operator using Helm. This command will create a new namespace called 'openfeature' and deploy the operator there:
```bash
helm install open-feature-operator open-feature-operator/open-feature-operator --namespace openfeature --create-namespace --version 0.8.9
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the OpenFeature operator pod is running:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n openfeature
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
Configure resource limits for the OpenFeature operator to ensure it runs efficiently:
```bash
kubectl set resources deployment open-feature-operator --namespace openfeature --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi --requests=cpu=250m,memory=256Mi
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the OpenFeature operator using Helm:
```bash
helm uninstall open-feature-operator --namespace openfeature
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs
uninstall.description:
Remove any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) associated with OpenFeature:
```bash
kubectl delete crd featureflags.openfeature.dev
kubectl delete crd featureflagproviders.openfeature.dev
```

uninstall.title: Remove the namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'openfeature' namespace to clean up any remaining resources:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace openfeature
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current configuration of the OpenFeature operator:
```bash
kubectl get all -n openfeature -o yaml > openfeature-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Ensure your Helm repository is updated to get the latest charts:
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade OpenFeature
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the OpenFeature operator to the latest version:
```bash
helm upgrade open-feature-operator open-feature-operator/open-feature-operator --namespace openfeature --version 0.8.9
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the OpenFeature operator pod is running after the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n openfeature
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
If your OpenFeature pods are stuck in a Pending state, check for insufficient resources in your cluster:
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> -n openfeature
``` 
Ensure that your cluster has enough CPU and memory resources available.

troubleshooting.title: OpenFeature operator pod crashes
troubleshooting.description:
If the OpenFeature operator pod is crashing, check the logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n openfeature
``` 
Look for any configuration issues or missing dependencies.

troubleshooting.title: Helm installation fails
troubleshooting.description:
If the Helm installation fails, check for existing resources that may conflict:
```bash
kubectl get all -n openfeature
``` 
Ensure there are no existing deployments or services with the same name.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of OpenFeature will have the operator running in the 'openfeature' namespace with all pods in the Running state. You can verify this by checking the status of the pods using kubectl.
codeSnippets: kubectl set resources deployment open-feature-operator --namespace openfeature --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi --requests=cpu=250m,memory=256Mi

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster and Helm installed to deploy OpenFeature.
